children.heading: Small business intermediary lending pilot program
children.label: l Small business intermediary lending pilot program
I
children.text: seeks or has been awarded a loan from the Administrator to make loans to small business concerns under this subsection; and

children.label: II
children.text: has not less than 1 year of experience making loans to startup, newly established, or growing small business concerns; and

children.label: I
children.text: a private, nonprofit community development corporation;

children.label: II
children.text: a consortium of private, nonprofit organizations or nonprofit community development corporations; and

children.label: III
children.text: an agency of or nonprofit entity established by a Native American Tribal Government; and

children.label: B
children.text: the term “Program” means the small business intermediary lending pilot program established under paragraph (2).

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 2 Establishment
children.text: There is established a 3-year small business intermediary lending pilot program, under which the Administrator may make direct loans to eligible intermediaries, for the purpose of making loans to startup, newly established, and growing small business concerns.

children.label: A
children.text: to assist small business concerns in areas suffering from a lack of credit due to poor economic conditions or changes in the financial market; and

children.label: B
children.text: to establish a loan program under which the Administrator may provide loans to eligible intermediaries to enable the eligible intermediaries to provide loans to startup, newly established, and growing small business concerns for working capital, real estate, or the acquisition of materials, supplies, or equipment.

children.label: i
children.text: the type of small business concerns to be assisted;

children.label: ii
children.text: the size and range of loans to be made;

children.label: iii
children.text: the interest rate and terms of loans to be made;

children.label: iv
children.text: the geographic area to be served and the economic, poverty, and unemployment characteristics of the area;

children.label: v
children.text: the status of small business concerns in the area to be served and an analysis of the availability of credit; and

children.label: vi
children.text: the qualifications of the applicant to carry out this subsection.

children.heading: Loan limits
children.label: B Loan limits
children.text: No loan may be made to an eligible intermediary under this subsection if the total amount outstanding and committed to the eligible intermediary by the Administrator would, as a result of such loan, exceed $1,000,000 during the participation of the eligible intermediary in the Program.

children.heading: Loan duration
children.label: C Loan duration
children.text: Loans made by the Administrator under this subsection shall be for a term of 20 years.

children.heading: Applicable interest rates
children.label: D Applicable interest rates
children.text: Loans made by the Administrator to an eligible intermediary under the Program shall bear an annual interest rate equal to 1.00 percent.

children.heading: Fees; collateral
children.label: E Fees; collateral
children.text: The Administrator may not charge any fees or require collateral with respect to any loan made to an eligible intermediary under this subsection.

children.heading: Delayed payments
children.label: F Delayed payments
children.text: The Administrator shall not require the repayment of principal or interest on a loan made to an eligible intermediary under the Program during the 2-year period beginning on the date of the initial disbursement of funds under that loan.

children.label: i
children.text: to not more than 20 eligible intermediaries; and

children.label: ii
children.text: in a total amount of not more than $20,000,000.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Administrator, through an eligible intermediary, shall make loans to startup, newly established, and growing small business concerns for working capital, real estate, and the acquisition of materials, supplies, furniture, fixtures, and equipment.

children.heading: Maximum loan
children.label: B Maximum loan
children.text: An eligible intermediary may not make a loan under this subsection of more than $200,000 to any 1 small business concern.

children.heading: Applicable interest rates
children.label: C Applicable interest rates
children.text: A loan made by an eligible intermediary to a small business concern under this subsection, may have a fixed or a variable interest rate, and shall bear an interest rate specified by the eligible intermediary in the application of the eligible intermediary for a loan under this subsection.

children.heading: Review restrictions
children.label: D Review restrictions
children.text: The Administrator may not review individual loans made by an eligible intermediary to a small business concern before approval of the loan by the eligible intermediary.

children.heading: Termination
children.label: 6 Termination
children.text: The authority of the Administrator to make loans under the Program shall terminate 3 years after .September 27, 2010

children.label: i
children.text: to assist women, low-income, veteran (within the meaning of such term under ), and minority entrepreneurs and business owners and other such individuals possessing the capability to operate successful business concerns;section 632(q) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: to assist small business concerns in those areas suffering from a lack of credit due to economic downturns;

children.label: I
children.text: to make loans to eligible intermediaries to enable such intermediaries to provide small-scale loans, particularly loans in amounts averaging not more than $10,000, to startup, newly established, or growing small business concerns for working capital or the acquisition of materials, supplies, or equipment;

children.label: II
children.text: to make grants to eligible intermediaries that, together with non-Federal matching funds, will enable such intermediaries to provide intensive marketing, management, and technical assistance to microloan borrowers;

children.label: III
children.text: to make grants to eligible nonprofit entities that, together with non-Federal matching funds, will enable such entities to provide intensive marketing, management, and technical assistance to assist low-income entrepreneurs and other low-income individuals obtain private sector financing for their businesses, with or without loan guarantees; and

children.label: IV
children.text: to report to the Committees on Small Business of the Senate and the House of Representatives on the effectiveness of the microloan program and the advisability and feasibility of implementing such a program nationwide; and

children.label: I
children.text: establishing small businesses; and

children.label: II
children.text: eliminating their dependence on that assistance.

children.label: i
children.text: make direct loans to eligible intermediaries, as provided under paragraph (3), for the purpose of making short-term, fixed interest rate microloans to startup, newly established, and growing small business concerns under paragraph (6);

children.label: ii
children.text: in conjunction with such loans and subject to the requirements of paragraph (4), make grants to such intermediaries for the purpose of providing intensive marketing, management, and technical assistance to small business concerns that are borrowers under this subsection; and

children.label: iii
children.text: subject to the requirements of paragraph (5), make grants to nonprofit entities for the purpose of providing marketing, management, and technical assistance to low-income individuals seeking to start or enlarge their own businesses, if such assistance includes working with the grant recipient to secure loans in amounts not to exceed $50,000 from private sector lending institutions, with or without a loan guarantee from the nonprofit entity.

children.label: A
children.text: meets the definition in paragraph (10); and2

children.label: B
children.text: has at least 1 year of experience making microloans to startup, newly established, or growing small business concerns and providing, as an integral part of its microloan program, intensive marketing, management, and technical assistance to its borrowers.

children.label: I
children.text: the type of businesses to be assisted;

children.label: II
children.text: the size and range of loans to be made;

children.label: III
children.text: the geographic area to be served and its economic, poverty, and unemployment characteristics;

children.label: IV
children.text: the status of small business concerns in the area to be served and an analysis of their credit and technical assistance needs;

children.label: V
children.text: any marketing, management, and technical assistance to be provided in connection with a loan made under this subsection;

children.label: VI
children.text: the local economic credit markets, including the costs associated with obtaining credit locally;

children.label: VII
children.text: the qualifications of the applicant to carry out the purpose of this subsection; and

children.label: VIII
children.text: any plan to involve other technical assistance providers (such as counselors from the Service Corps of Retired Executives or small business development centers) or private sector lenders in assisting selected business concerns.

children.heading: Selection of intermediaries
children.label: ii Selection of intermediaries
children.text: In selecting intermediaries to participate in the program established under this subsection, the Administration shall give priority to those applicants that provide loans in amounts averaging not more than $10,000.

children.heading: Intermediary contribution
children.label: B Intermediary contribution
children.text: As a condition of any loan made to an intermediary under subparagraph (B)(i) of paragraph (1), the Administrator shall require the intermediary to contribute not less than 15 percent of the loan amount in cash from non-Federal sources.

children.heading: Loan limits
children.label: C Loan limits
children.text: Notwithstanding subsection (a)(3), no loan shall be made under this subsection if the total amount outstanding and committed to one intermediary (excluding outstanding grants) from the business loan and investment fund established by this chapter would, as a result of such loan, exceed $750,000 in the first year of such intermediary’s participation in the program, $7,000,000 (in the aggregate) in the remaining years of the intermediary’s participation in the program, and $3,000,000 in any of those remaining years.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Administrator shall, by regulation, require each intermediary to establish a loan loss reserve fund, and to maintain such reserve fund until all obligations owed to the Administration under this subsection are repaid.

children.heading: Level of loan loss reserve fund
children.label: ii Level of loan loss reserve fund

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: Subject to subclause (III), the Administrator shall require the loan loss reserve fund of an intermediary to be maintained at a level equal to 15 percent of the outstanding balance of the notes receivable owed to the intermediary.

children.heading: Review of loan loss reserve
children.label: II Review of loan loss reserve
children.text: After the initial 5 years of an intermediary’s participation in the program authorized by this subsection, the Administrator shall, at the request of the intermediary, conduct a review of the annual loss rate of the intermediary. Any intermediary in operation under this subsection prior to , that requests a reduction in its loan loss reserve shall be reviewed based on the most recent 5-year period preceding the request.October 1, 1994

children.heading: Reduction of loan loss reserve
children.label: III Reduction of loan loss reserve
children.text: Subject to the requirements of clause IV, the Administrator may reduce the annual loan loss reserve requirement of an intermediary to reflect the actual average loan loss rate for the intermediary during the preceding 5-year period, except that in no case shall the loan loss reserve be reduced to less than 10 percent of the outstanding balance of the notes receivable owed to the intermediary.19So in original. Probably should be “subclause (IV),”.19

children.label: aa
children.text: the average annual loss rate for the intermediary during the preceding 5-year period is less than 15 percent; and

children.label: bb
children.text: that no other factors exist that may impair the ability of the intermediary to repay all obligations owed to the Administration under this subsection.20So in original. The word “that” probably should not appear.20

children.heading: Unavailability of comparable credit
children.label: E Unavailability of comparable credit
children.text: An intermediary may make a loan under this subsection of more than $20,000 to a small business concern only if such small business concern demonstrates that it is unable to obtain credit elsewhere at comparable interest rates and that it has good prospects for success. In no case shall an intermediary make a loan under this subsection of more than $50,000, or have outstanding or committed to any 1 borrower more than $50,000.

children.heading: Loan duration
children.label: i Loan duration
children.text: Loans made by the Administration under this subsection shall be for a term of 10 years.

children.heading: Applicable interest rates
children.label: ii Applicable interest rates
children.text: Except as provided in clause (iii), loans made by the Administration under this subsection to an intermediary shall bear an interest rate equal to 1.25 percentage points below the rate determined by the Secretary of the Treasury for obligations of the United States with a period of maturity of 5 years, adjusted to the nearest one-eighth of 1 percent.

children.heading: Rates applicable to certain small loans
children.label: iii Rates applicable to certain small loans
children.text: Loans made by the Administration to an intermediary that makes loans to small business concerns and entrepreneurs averaging not more than $7,500, shall bear an interest rate that is 2 percentage points below the rate determined by the Secretary of the Treasury for obligations of the United States with a period of maturity of 5 years, adjusted to the nearest one-eighth of 1 percent.

children.heading: Rates applicable to multiple sites or offices
children.label: iv Rates applicable to multiple sites or offices
children.text: The interest rate prescribed in clause (ii) or (iii) shall apply to each separate loan-making site or office of 1 intermediary only if such site or office meets the requirements of that clause.

children.label: I
children.text: be applied retroactively for the first year of an intermediary’s participation in the program, based upon the actual lending practices of the intermediary as determined by the Administration prior to the end of such year; and

children.label: II
children.text: be based in the second and subsequent years of an intermediary’s participation in the program, upon the actual lending practices of the intermediary during the term of the intermediary’s participation in the program.

children.heading: Covered intermediaries21So in original. Probably should be “(vi)”.21
children.label: vii Covered intermediaries21So in original. Probably should be “(vi)”.21
children.text: The interest rates prescribed in this subparagraph shall apply to all loans made to intermediaries under this subsection on or after .October 28, 1991

children.heading: Delayed payments
children.label: G Delayed payments
children.text: The Administration shall not require repayment of interest or principal of a loan made to an intermediary under this subsection during the first year of the loan.

children.heading: Fees; collateral
children.label: H Fees; collateral
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraphs (B) and (D), the Administration shall not charge any fees or require collateral other than an assignment of the notes receivable of the microloans with respect to any loan made to an intermediary under this subsection.

children.heading: Marketing, management and technical assistance grants to intermediaries
children.label: 4 Marketing, management and technical assistance grants to intermediaries

children.heading: Grant amounts
children.label: A Grant amounts
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in subparagraphs (C) and (G) and subject to subparagraph (B), each intermediary that receives a loan under subparagraph (B)(i) of paragraph (1) shall be eligible to receive a grant to provide marketing, management, and technical assistance to small business concerns that are borrowers under this subsection. Except as provided in subparagraphs (C) and (G), each intermediary meeting the requirements of subparagraph (B) may receive a grant of not more than 25 percent of the total outstanding balance of loans made to it under this subsection.

children.heading: Contribution
children.label: B Contribution
children.text: As a condition of a grant made under subparagraph (A), the Administrator shall require the intermediary to contribute an amount equal to 25 percent of the amount of the grant, obtained solely from non-Federal sources. In addition to cash or other direct funding, the contribution may include indirect costs or in-kind contributions paid for under non-Federal programs.

children.heading: Additional technical assistance grants for making certain loans
children.label: C Additional technical assistance grants for making certain loans
I
children.text: the intermediary provides not less than 25 percent of its loans to small business concerns located in or owned by 1 or more residents of an economically distressed area; or

children.label: aa
children.text: that averages not more than $10,000 during the period of the intermediary’s participation in the program; or

children.label: bb
children.text: of which not less than 25 percent is serving rural areas during the period of the intermediary’s participation in the program.

children.heading: Purposes
children.label: ii Purposes
children.text: A grant awarded under clause (i) may be used to provide marketing, management, and technical assistance to small business concerns that are borrowers under this subsection.

children.heading: Contribution exception
children.label: iii Contribution exception
children.text: The contribution requirements in subparagraph (B) do not apply to grants made under this subparagraph.

children.heading: Eligibility for multiple sites or offices
children.label: D Eligibility for multiple sites or offices
children.text: The eligibility for a grant described in subparagraph (A), or (C) shall be determined separately for each loan-making site or office of 1 intermediary.9

children.heading: Assistance to certain small business concerns
children.label: E Assistance to certain small business concerns

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Each intermediary may expend an amount not to exceed 50 percent of the grant funds received under paragraph (1)(B)(ii) to provide information and technical assistance to small business concerns that are prospective borrowers under this subsection.

children.heading: Technical assistance
children.label: ii Technical assistance
children.text: An intermediary may expend not more than 50 percent of the funds received under paragraph (1)(B)(ii) to enter into third party contracts for the provision of technical assistance.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Administration may accept any funds transferred to the Administration from other departments or agencies of the Federal Government to make grants in accordance with this subparagraph and section 202(b) of the Small Business Reauthorization Act of 1997 to participating intermediaries and technical assistance providers under paragraph (5), for use in accordance with clause (iii) to provide additional technical assistance and related services to recipients of assistance under a State program described in paragraph (1)(A)(iv) at the time they initially apply for assistance under this subparagraph.

children.heading: Eligible recipients; grant amounts
children.label: ii Eligible recipients; grant amounts
children.text: In making grants under this subparagraph, the Administration may select, from among participating intermediaries and technical assistance providers described in clause (i), not more than 20 grantees in fiscal year 1998, not more than 25 grantees in fiscal year 1999, and not more than 30 grantees in fiscal year 2000, each of whom may receive a grant under this subparagraph in an amount not to exceed $200,000 per year.

children.label: I
children.text: are in addition to other grants provided under this subsection and shall not require the contribution of matching amounts as a condition of eligibility; and

children.label: aa
children.text: to pay or reimburse a portion of child care and transportation costs of recipients of assistance described in clause (i), to the extent such costs are not otherwise paid by State block grants under the Child Care Development Block Grant Act of 1990 ( et seq.) or under part A of title IV of the Social Security Act ( et seq.); and42 U.S.C. 985842 U.S.C. 6012

children.label: bb
children.text: for marketing, management, and technical assistance to recipients of assistance described in clause (i).

children.label: I
children.text: specify the terms and conditions of the grants under this subparagraph; and

children.label: II
children.text: provide for appropriate monitoring of expenditures by each grantee under this subparagraph and each recipient of assistance described in clause (i) who receives assistance from a grantee under this subparagraph, in order to ensure compliance with this subparagraph by those grantees and recipients of assistance.

children.heading: Grant amounts based on appropriations
children.label: G Grant amounts based on appropriations
children.text: In any fiscal year in which the amount appropriated to make grants under subparagraph (A) is sufficient to provide to each intermediary that receives a loan under paragraph (1)(B)(i) a grant of not less than 25 percent of the total outstanding balance of loans made to the intermediary under this subsection, the Administration shall make a grant under subparagraph (A) to each intermediary of not less than 25 percent and not more than 30 percent of that total outstanding balance for the intermediary.

children.heading: Private sector borrowing technical assistance grants
children.label: 5 Private sector borrowing technical assistance grants

children.heading: Grant amounts
children.label: A Grant amounts
children.text: Subject to the requirements of subparagraph (B), the Administration may make not more than 55 grants annually, each in amounts not to exceed $200,000 for the purposes specified in subparagraph (B)(iii) of paragraph (1).

children.heading: Contribution
children.label: B Contribution
children.text: As a condition of any grant made under subparagraph (A), the Administration shall require the grant recipient to contribute an amount equal to 20 percent of the amount of the grant, obtained solely from non-Federal sources. In addition to cash or other direct funding, the contribution may include indirect costs or in-kind contributions paid for under non-Federal programs.

children.heading: Loans to small business concerns from eligible intermediaries
children.label: 6 Loans to small business concerns from eligible intermediaries

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: An eligible intermediary shall make short-term, fixed rate loans to startup, newly established, and growing small business concerns from the funds made available to it under subparagraph (B)(i) of paragraph (1) for working capital and the acquisition of materials, supplies, furniture, fixtures, and equipment.

children.heading: Portfolio requirement
children.label: B Portfolio requirement
children.text: To the extent practicable, each intermediary that operates a microloan program under this subsection shall maintain a microloan portfolio with an average loan size of not more than $15,000.

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of a loan of more than $7,500 made by the intermediary to a small business concern or entrepreneur by more than 7.75 percentage points; and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a loan of not more than $7,500 made by the intermediary to a small business concern or entrepreneur by more than 8.5 percentage points.

children.heading: Review restriction
children.label: D Review restriction
children.text: The Administration shall not review individual microloans made by intermediaries prior to approval.

children.heading: Establishment of child care or transportation businesses
children.label: E Establishment of child care or transportation businesses
children.text: In addition to other eligible small businesses concerns, borrowers under any program under this subsection may include individuals who will use the loan proceeds to establish for-profit or nonprofit child care establishments or businesses providing for-profit transportation services.

children.heading: Number of participants
children.label: A Number of participants
children.text: Under the program authorized by this subsection, the Administration may fund, on a competitive basis, not more than 300 intermediaries.

children.label: bb
children.text: ⁄ of the total amount of new loan funds made available for award under this subsection for that fiscal year; and155

children.label: II
children.text: any additional amount, as determined by the Administration.

children.heading: Redistribution
children.label: ii Redistribution
children.text: If, at the beginning of the third quarter of a fiscal year, the Administration determines that any portion of the amount made available to carry out this subsection is unlikely to be made available under clause (i) during that fiscal year, the Administration may make that portion available for award in any one or more States (including the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the United States Virgin Islands, Guam, and American Samoa) without regard to clause (i).

children.heading: Equitable distribution of intermediaries
children.label: 8 Equitable distribution of intermediaries
children.text: In approving microloan program applicants and providing funding to intermediaries under this subsection, the Administration shall select and provide funding to such intermediaries as will ensure appropriate availability of loans for small businesses in all industries located throughout each State, particularly those located in urban and in rural areas.

children.heading: Grants for management, marketing, technical assistance, and related services
children.label: 9 Grants for management, marketing, technical assistance, and related services

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Administration may procure technical assistance for intermediaries participating in the Microloan Program to ensure that such intermediaries have the knowledge, skills, and understanding of microlending practices necessary to operate successful microloan programs.

children.heading: Assistance amount
children.label: B Assistance amount
children.text: The Administration shall transfer 7 percent of its annual appropriation for loans and loan guarantees under this subsection to the Administration’s Salaries and Expense Account for the specific purpose of providing 1 or more technical assistance grants to experienced microlending organizations and national and regional nonprofit organizations that have demonstrated experience in providing training support for microenterprise development and financing. to achieve the purpose set forth in subparagraph (A).22So in original. The period probably should not appear.22

children.heading: Welfare-to-work microloan initiative
children.label: C Welfare-to-work microloan initiative
children.text: Of amounts made available to carry out the welfare-to-work microloan initiative under paragraph (1)(A)(iv) in any fiscal year, the Administration may use not more than 5 percent to provide technical assistance, either directly or through contractors, to welfare-to-work microloan initiative grantees, to ensure that, as grantees, they have the knowledge, skills, and understanding of microlending and welfare-to-work transition, and other related issues, to operate a successful welfare-to-work microloan initiative.

children.label: A
children.text: the numbers and locations of the intermediaries funded to conduct microloan programs;

children.label: B
children.text: the amounts of each loan and each grant to intermediaries;

children.label: C
children.text: a description of the matching contributions of each intermediary;

children.label: D
children.text: the numbers and amounts of microloans made by the intermediaries to small business concern borrowers;

children.label: E
children.text: the repayment history of each intermediary;

children.label: F
children.text: a description of the loan portfolio of each intermediary including the extent to which it provides microloans to small business concerns in rural areas; and

children.label: G
children.text: any recommendations for legislative changes that would improve program operations.

children.label: i
children.text: a private, nonprofit entity;

children.label: ii
children.text: a private, nonprofit community development corporation;

children.label: iii
children.text: a consortium of private, nonprofit organizations or nonprofit community development corporations;

children.label: I
children.text: no application is received from an eligible nonprofit organization; or

children.label: II
children.text: the Administration determines that the needs of a region or geographic area are not adequately served by an existing, eligible nonprofit organization that has submitted an application; or

children.label: v
children.text: an agency of or nonprofit entity established by a Native American Tribal Government,

children.label: B
children.text: the term “microloan” means a short-term, fixed rate loan of not more than $50,000, made by an intermediary to a startup, newly established, or growing small business concern;

children.label: i
children.text: in a nonmetropolitan county (as defined by the Secretary of Agriculture) or its equivalent thereof; or

children.label: ii
children.text: in a metropolitan county or its equivalent that has a resident population of less than 20,000 if the Small Business Administration has determined such political subdivision or area to be rural; and

children.label: D
children.text: the term “economically distressed area”, as used in paragraph (4), means a county or equivalent division of local government of a State in which the small business concern is located, in which, according to the most recent data available from the Bureau of the Census, Department of Commerce, not less than 40 percent of residents have an annual income that is at or below the poverty level.

children.heading: Number of loans
children.label: A Number of loans
children.text: In carrying out this paragraph, the Administration shall not participate in providing financing on a deferred basis to more than 10 intermediaries in urban areas or more than 10 intermediaries in rural areas.

children.heading: Term of loans
children.label: B Term of loans
children.text: The term of each loan shall be 10 years. During the first year of the loan, the intermediary shall not be required to repay any interest or principal. During the second through fifth years of the loan, the intermediary shall be required to pay interest only. During the sixth through tenth years of the loan, the intermediary shall be required to make interest payments and fully amortize the principal.

children.heading: Interest rate
children.label: C Interest rate
children.text: The interest rate on each loan shall be the rate specified by paragraph (3)(F) for direct loans.

children.heading: Evaluation of welfare-to-work microloan initiative
children.label: 13 Evaluation of welfare-to-work microloan initiative
children.text: On , and annually thereafter, the Administration shall submit to the Committees on Small Business of the House of Representatives and the Senate a report on any monies distributed pursuant to paragraph (4)(F).January 31, 1999

children.heading: Repayment deferred for active service reservists
children.label: n Repayment deferred for active service reservists

children.heading: Active service
children.label: A Active service
children.text: The term “active service” has the meaning given that term in .section 101(d)(3) of title 10

children.heading: Eligible reservist
children.label: B Eligible reservist
children.text: The term “eligible reservist” means a member of a reserve component of the Armed Forces ordered to perform active service for a period of more than 30 consecutive days.

children.heading: Essential employee
children.label: C Essential employee
children.text: The term “essential employee” means an individual who is employed by a small business concern and whose managerial or technical expertise is critical to the successful day-to-day operations of that small business concern.

children.label: i
children.text: an individual who is an eligible reservist and who received a direct loan under subsection (a) or (b) before being ordered to active service; or

children.label: ii
children.text: a small business concern that received a direct loan under subsection (a) or (b) before an eligible reservist, who is an essential employee, was ordered to active service.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Administration shall, upon written request, defer repayment of principal and interest due on a direct loan made under subsection (a) or (b), if such loan was incurred by a qualified borrower.

children.heading: Period of deferral
children.label: B Period of deferral
children.text: The period of deferral for repayment under this paragraph shall begin on the date on which the eligible reservist is ordered to active service and shall terminate on the date that is 180 days after the date such eligible reservist is discharged or released from active service.

children.heading: Interest rate reduction during deferral
children.label: C Interest rate reduction during deferral
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, during the period of deferral described in subparagraph (B), the Administration may, in its discretion, reduce the interest rate on any loan qualifying for a deferral under this paragraph.

children.heading: Deferral of loan guarantees and other financings
children.label: 3 Deferral of loan guarantees and other financings
A
children.text: encourage intermediaries participating in the program under subsection (m) to defer repayment of a loan made with proceeds made available under that subsection, if such loan was incurred by a small business concern that is eligible to apply for assistance under subsection (b)(3); and

children.label: i
children.text: encourage lenders and other intermediaries to defer repayment of, or provide other relief relating to, loan guarantees under subsection (a) and financings under that were incurred by small business concerns that are eligible to apply for assistance under subsection (b)(3), and loan guarantees provided under subsection (m) if the intermediary provides relief to a small business concern under this paragraph; andsection 697a of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: implement a program to provide for the deferral of repayment or other relief to any intermediary providing relief to a small business borrower under this paragraph.
